reforms.description: The Swiss government aims to promote entrepreneurship by easing impediments to starting a business, including reducing the number of procedures and time required, and streamlining bankruptcy rules.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By reducing barriers to entry for new firms, the reform is expected to enhance innovation and productivity growth in the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has initiated measures to streamline business start-up processes.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform addresses significant barriers to entrepreneurship, which can lead to increased innovation and economic dynamism.
reforms.source_quote: More needs to be done to ease impediments to starting a business, including by reducing the number of procedures and the time required.

reforms.description: The Federal Council announced measures to promote energy research, including an additional CHF 202 million in public energy R&D funding over the 2013-16 period, which represents a 25% increase. This funding will create research networks between higher education institutions and the Swiss Competence Centres for Energy Research (SCCERs) to pursue research in seven action areas.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased public funding for energy R&D is expected to enhance technological advancements and innovation in the energy sector, contributing to long-term economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Parliament voted for the additional funding, indicating it has been enacted.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant increase in public investment in energy R&D, which is crucial for achieving the ambitious goals set out in the Energy Strategy 2050.
reforms.source_quote: In March 2013, Parliament voted an additional CHF 202 million in public energy R&D over the 2013-16 period (that is a 25% increase).

reforms.description: The Swiss government plans to enhance policies and programs that promote innovation and R&D, recognizing their significant positive spillovers to the broader community.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By fostering innovation and R&D, the policy aims to stimulate productivity and economic growth through technological advancements.
reforms.status_evidence: The government plans to shift towards renewable energy and enhance technology frontiers through active involvement.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a structural change in the approach to innovation and R&D, aiming to leverage technological advancements for sustainable growth.
reforms.source_quote: Policies and programmes that promote innovation and R&D are important, given their significant positive spillovers to the broader community.

reforms.description: The Swiss government aims to foster the emergence of more dynamic start-ups to drive innovation across various sectors, particularly focusing on supporting women entrepreneurs.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Encouraging start-ups can enhance competition and innovation, leading to increased productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Switzerland could do more to foster the emergence of more such firms that can drive innovation in a broader array of emerging sectors.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative is significant as it targets the growth of start-ups, which are crucial for innovation and economic dynamism.
reforms.source_quote: Entrepreneurship and the emergence of small dynamic start-ups are important drivers of innovation and productivity growth.

reforms.description: The Swiss government increased spending on innovation-related programmes by approximately CHF48 million as part of its fiscal response to the global financial crisis, which included additional funding for the Swiss National Science Foundation and the Commission for Technology and Innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased funding for research and innovation is expected to enhance the innovative capacity of firms, leading to higher productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government made additional expenditures on research and innovation as part of its fiscal response.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant increase in public investment in innovation, which is crucial for maintaining Switzerland's competitive edge in R&D and fostering long-term economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: As part of its fiscal response to the global financial crisis, the government increased spending on innovation-related programmes, making additional expenditures of about CHF48 million on research and innovation.

reforms.description: The Swiss government announced measures to promote energy research, including the creation of research networks between higher education institutions and the Swiss Competence Centres for Energy Research to advance energy technology.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Promoting energy research is expected to lead to technological advancements that can enhance energy efficiency and sustainability, contributing to long-term economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The Federal Council recently announced measures to promote energy research.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative is significant as it aims to address the critical area of energy technology, which is essential for Switzerland's transition to renewable energy sources.
reforms.source_quote: The Federal Council recently announced measures to promote energy research.

reforms.description: The CTI Entrepreneurship Programme provides university graduates and professionals with coaching and training to create companies, while the CTI Start-up Programme offers individual coaching for founders to secure funding and enter the market.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These programmes enhance the skills and resources available to new entrepreneurs, fostering innovation and job creation.
reforms.status_evidence: The CTI offers various assistance programmes for entrepreneurship training.
reforms.importance_rationale: These initiatives represent a significant effort to promote entrepreneurship and innovation among new graduates and professionals, contributing to the overall dynamism of the economy.
reforms.source_quote: The CTI Entrepreneurship Programme is intended for university graduates and professionals with interesting business ideas and provides them with coaching and training on how to create a company.

reforms.description: The government is advised to streamline procedures for starting a business by reducing the number of required procedures and time, which could enhance the role of small firms in innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Simplifying business start-up procedures can lower barriers to entry, encouraging new firm formation and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: Easing restrictions on starting a business by reducing the number of procedures and time required is recommended.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform addresses significant barriers to entrepreneurship, which is crucial for fostering innovation and economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: Easing restrictions on starting a business by reducing the number of procedures and time required might promote a greater role by dynamic small firms in innovation.
